original_text:
### Hierarchy & Application
- Apply the palette in the **primary > secondary > tertiary ratio** shown in the guide: blues and golds dominate; tertiary colors are accents and surfaces.
- **Headlines/headings:** Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).
- **Body text:** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2).
- **Charts/about:blank** Brand Blue + Sky Blue dominant; **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**; gray for placebo.
- **Callout surfaces:** Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).
- **Coral:** sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data. **[GENERAL]:** avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief calls for it.
- **Links [GENERAL]:** Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.
- The logo icon's **70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances** (logo, and when the mark is used as a pattern/graphic).

relations.note: The Coral restriction operationalizes its role as a tertiary accent within the palette hierarchy.
The callout treatments apply tertiary colors as surfaces.
The chart palette specifies dominance and accent use for data visualization.

quotes: Apply the palette in the **primary > secondary > tertiary ratio** shown in the guide: blues and golds dominate; tertiary colors are accents and surfaces.

rules.intent: Maintain the intended color hierarchy across brand materials.
rules.rule_text: Apply the palette in the primary > secondary > tertiary ratio shown in the guide: blues and golds dominate, while tertiary colors serve as accents and surfaces.

quotes: **Headlines/headings:** Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).
**Headlines/headings:** Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).

rules.intent: Preserve legible, consistent heading hierarchy across light and dark treatments.
rules.rule_text: Headlines and headings use Brand Blue (#00529b); use White (#ffffff) on dark or gradient backgrounds.

quotes: **Body text:** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2).
**Body text:** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2).

rules.intent: Ensure readable body copy on both light and dark surfaces.
rules.rule_text: Body text uses Graphite (#212121) on light backgrounds. On dark backgrounds, use White (#ffffff) with increased line height.

quotes: **Charts/about:blank** Brand Blue + Sky Blue dominant; **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**; gray for placebo.
**Charts/about:blank** Brand Blue + Sky Blue dominant; **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**; gray for placebo.

rules.intent: Keep data visualizations clear and ensure emphasis colors retain meaning.
rules.rule_text: In charts, Brand Blue (#00529b) and Sky Blue (#358ccb) are dominant; Sunshine (#faa31b) and Gold (#ffc60a) are strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures, and gray denotes placebo.

quotes: **Callout surfaces:** Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).
**Callout surfaces:** Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).

rules.intent: Differentiate data-focused and text-heavy callout content consistently.
rules.rule_text: Data callouts use Light Blue (#e6f0f9) at 70% tint; text-heavy callouts use Golden Sand (#fef1c8) at 30% tint.

quotes: **Coral:** sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data.
**Coral:** sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data.

rules.intent: Prevent Coral from competing with primary brand and data-emphasis colors.
rules.rule_text: Use Coral (#f26a38) only as a sparing tertiary accent; never use it for primary CTAs, headlines, or data.

quotes: **[GENERAL]:** avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief calls for it.

rules.intent: Keep email color treatments restrained unless Coral is specifically briefed.
rules.rule_text: Avoid Coral (#f26a38) in email sections unless the brief calls for it.

quotes: **Links [GENERAL]:** Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.
**Links [GENERAL]:** Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.

rules.intent: Maintain recognizable, legible links across background treatments.
rules.rule_text: Links are underlined and use Brand Blue (#00529b) on light backgrounds; use underlined White (#ffffff) on dark backgrounds.

quotes: The logo icon's **70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances** (logo, and when the mark is used as a pattern/graphic).
The logo icon's **70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances** (logo, and when the mark is used as a pattern/graphic).

rules.intent: Protect consistent logo-mark rendering wherever the mark appears.
rules.rule_text: Maintain the logo icon at 70% transparency in Gold (Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a) in every instance, including the logo and mark-based patterns or graphics.

quotes: - **Body text:** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2).
- **Callout surfaces:** Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).
- **Links [GENERAL]:** Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.

rules.intent: Bind the Hierarchy & Application table values verbatim.
rules.rule_text:
The Hierarchy & Application table is a closed, brand-approved set; every row binds its listed values exactly as specified. Do not invent or alter rows.

| item | value |
|---|---|
| Body text | ** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2). |
| Callout surfaces | ** Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts). |
| Links [GENERAL] | ** Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark. |
